assertions.expectation: Configures a recurring maintenance window for weekends only
assertions.reason: Provides weekend maintenance window configuration with Saturday 2-6 AM schedule using FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA recurrence pattern

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start/end and --maintenance-window-recurrence for weekly weekend schedule
assertions.reason: Uses --maintenance-window-start and --maintenance-window-recurrence but does not use --maintenance-window-end flag; instead uses --maintenance-window-duration which is different syntax

assertions.expectation: Sets up maintenance exclusions for quarterly code freezes (up to 30 days each)
assertions.reason: Provides specific example for Q4 code freeze (December 15 - January 15, 30 days) with proper gcloud command syntax and mentions quarterly freezes

assertions.expectation: Sets up a maintenance exclusion for the November audit period
assertions.reason: Provides specific November 1-30 audit exclusion with complete gcloud command including proper start/end dates

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for maintenance exclusions and how to handle longer freezes
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Each exclusion limited to 30 days maximum' and provides example of chaining exclusions for consecutive freeze periods

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: Does not explicitly explain this distinction; shows manual upgrade commands but doesn't clarify that they bypass maintenance windows

assertions.expectation: Recommends combining maintenance windows with Stable or Extended channel for maximum upgrade control
assertions.reason: Recommends Extended channel strategy and explains how it provides maximum control over upgrades while maintaining security patches

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for each exclusion (--add-maintenance-exclusion-name/start-time/end-time)
assertions.reason: Provides complete gcloud commands with --add-maintenance-exclusion-name, --add-maintenance-exclusion-start, and --add-maintenance-exclusion-end flags for both quarterly and audit exclusions

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during long exclusion periods
assertions.reason: Does not explicitly warn about version drift risks; focuses on benefits of control but doesn't address the compliance/security risks of falling behind on versions
